items.title: Biotech’s IPO tide is finally turning, thanks in part to the recent M&A surge
items.snippet: After years of suffering from a bear market and more than 14 months of geopolitical turmoil shaking the macroenvironment, biotech appears to be moving on.

items.title: BMS, Hengrui build 13-asset pipeline in $15B+ onco-immune deal
items.snippet: Of the 13 programs that the companies will advance, four will come from Hengrui Pharma and four from Bristol Myers Squibb. The remaining five assets will be jointly discovered.

items.title: BridgeBio stands up to Pfizer in ATTR-CM as late-stage data hint at better survival
items.snippet: Indirect comparisons between BridgeBio’s Attruby and Pfizer’s tafamidis products showed a numerical survival benefit with the biotech’s drug.

items.title: FDA turns to old drugs to address new diseases, persistent medical needs
items.snippet: The FDA is looking to retool drugs with sufficient evidence to support their use in other indications, particularly those with unmet need.

items.title: Daiichi takes $850M charge, axes facility investment as ADC demand forecast falls
items.snippet: While Daiichi Sankyo brought in $13.4 billion in 2025, setbacks forced the company to update its antibody-drug conjugate forecast, pushing demand below the minimum supply agreed upon with CMOs and prompting the cancellation of an in-house investment.

items.title: Makary’s reported FDA removal could be 'broad positive' for biopharma
items.snippet: The widely covered impending ouster of FDA Commissioner Marty Makary not long after the exit of controversial biologics head Vinay Prasad highlights the severe turnover rates at the highest rungs of leadership at the health department.

items.title: Partner’s Bizengri cleared by FDA for ultra-rare cancer days after winning priority voucher
items.snippet: Partner Therapeutics announced last week that the FDA had granted Bizengri a Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher to accelerate drug review.

items.title: Advances in RSV Vaccine Research and Development
items.snippet: Epidemiology, Next-Generation Vaccine Platforms, and the Critical Role of Prefusion F Antigens in RSV Research

items.title: Merck, Amgen double down on bad cholesterol to vanquish number 1 killer
items.snippet: New guidelines from two leading medical associations suggest that efforts to reduce bad cholesterol should focus on maintaining low levels of two key lipoproteins. Big pharma is all in, looking to improve on the standard statins to help vanquish America's number one killer: heart disease.

items.title: Published FDA rejections point to manufacturing, data gaps as key stumbling blocks
items.snippet: The FDA's decision last year to make complete response letters public provides new insight into why therapies sometimes fail to get the regulatory greenlight. Analysts say the information could help sponsors refine their regulatory strategies.

items.title: Trump signs off on plan to oust embattled FDA Commissioner Marty Makary: report
items.snippet: If ultimately confirmed, Makary’s planned departure, broken by The Wall Street Journal Friday afternoon, would follow a controversial tenure in which his deputy and constant co-author Vinay Prasad riled biopharma feathers with myriad unexpected drug rejections. Prasad stepped down as biologics chief last week.

items.title: Biogen, Eisai hit with 3-month delay for starting SubQ Alzheimer’s therapy
items.snippet: The FDA’s extension will give reviewers more time to review a major amendment to Biogen and Eisai’s application for a subcutaneous induction formulation of Alzheimer’s therapy Leqembi. The new target action date is on Aug. 24.

items.title: Lilly invests $4.5B in Indiana manufacturing, opens doors of 1st genetic medicine site
items.snippet: Eli Lilly’s latest manufacturing expansion will support production of obesity blockbusters and next-gen assets, while the new Lilly Lebanon Advanced Therapies site will take experimental genetic medicines from research to commercialization.

items.title: Odyssey launches upsized $279M IPO, Seaport docks on Nasdaq
items.snippet: Analysts are cautiously optimistic about an IPO rebound for biopharma. BioSpace is keeping track of companies that seek to trade on the public markets this year.

items.title: Gilead laying off vast majority of Arcellx employees
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Gilead sets blockbuster bar for Yeztugo’s first full year on the market
items.snippet: With initial patients coming back for their second dose of Gilead’s twice-yearly PrEP injection, the pharma thinks the shot will hit $1 billion in sales this year, serving as “a cornerstone in Gilead's revenue growth story.”

items.title: Ascendis retreats to rare endocrinology wheelhouse as cancer plans falter
items.snippet: Before discontinuing the asset, Ascendis Pharma was studying onvapegleukin alfa in advanced or metastatic solid tumors, demonstrating a three- to four-month overall survival advantage over historical controls.

items.title: FDA pilots AI-informed one-day facility inspections
items.snippet: Implemented as part of a broader initiative to make more targeted and efficient use of inspection resources, the pilot program will use artificial intelligence to enable shorter, focused screenings to complement standard FDA inspections.

items.title: FDA reverses course on Atara, Pierre Fabre’s twice-rejected cell therapy after Prasad’s exit
items.snippet: The FDA turned away Ebvallo in January, taking issue with the design of the registrational trial. In a recent meeting, however, the agency agreed that the study could in fact support the cell therapy’s approval. The news comes a week after the departure of controversial biologics Director Vinay Prasad.

items.title: Angelini inks $4.1B Catalyst buy to enter US rare disease market
items.snippet: Catalyst Pharmaceuticals comes with a trio of approved drugs as Angelini Pharma expands its neuroscience offerings.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.

items.title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
items.snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.
